last_result.effect: This asks the manager for information only; it does not change the EasyCrypt proof state.
last_result.result: The manager returned read-only context for the current goal.

content.preview: [WHERE-HIT] mu_mem_le_mu1_size (kind: lemma) * In [lemmas or axioms]: lemma mu_mem_le_mu1_size ['a]: forall (dt : 'a Distr.distr) (l : 'a list) (r : real) (n : int), size l <= n => (forall (x : 'a), mu1 dt x <= r) => mu dt (mem l) <= n%r * r.

source.label: Parsed from EasyCrypt pretty goal text

navigation.confidence_reason: The current probability goal has a product bound; the ledger is partial but still useful for avoiding local-budget traps.
navigation.why_now: Treat the product bound as a resource ledger before committing sampling, split, seq, or direct call steps.

assertion_must_contain.next_after_boundary: return to the list-size times point-mass ledger; the boundary does not discharge the product budget

allowed_boundary_moves.safe_when: Use only when the call proof obligation preserves the same membership event across the abstract call frontier.

route_plan: decompose product budget into factor obligations
descend to sampling only after per-point bound is explicit

anti_routes.reason: A single accepted `rnd` step, or a compact `do N!rnd`, may leave a subgoal that charges local samples against the entire product budget.
EasyCrypt may accept `phoare split !`, but the residual goals can still be a bad route under a product budget.
A plausible `seq K : side_fact` can move the small product budget onto a high-probability prefix or deterministic side condition.

navigation.next_after_boundary: If you use an event-preserving seq/call boundary, keep the product budget on the membership event and return to the list-size times point-mass route.

repair_if_fails.next: inspect probability_budget_ledger before committing another rnd/seq/split route
use undo_to_checkpoint to return before the budget-misallocated seq/split

navigation.confidence_reason: The current goal is a single-program Pr[...] state and the current view exposes probability/phoare route evidence. The fast-track tactic is schematic, so confidence is capped until the current view or an inspect result supplies concrete terms.
navigation.why_now: current goal contains a single Pr[...] probability expression; only one Pr[...] endpoint is visible; candidate/context evidence mentions byphoare, phoare, or probability frontiers.

fast_track_probe.tactic: byphoare (_: <pre> ==> <post>) => //; proc; inline *; wp; sp; while (INV) (VARIANT).
preconditions: Use after the current branch/goal has a concrete single-program probability obligation.
Fill <pre>, <post>, INV, and VARIANT from the visible goal/program state.
For phoare loops, use exactly `while (INV) (VARIANT).`; do not add a colon or probability argument.
Probe the bounded block first; commit only the accepted block or accepted prefix.
fast_track_probe.expected_next_shape: A phoare/probability loop obligation, or residual invariant/base subgoals that can be repaired locally.

anti_routes.reason: This is not currently a relational Pr equality with two endpoints; starting with byequiv usually searches the wrong proof family.

repair_if_fails.next: inspect tactic_forms for byphoare/phoare while forms
inspect goal_info before choosing the probability route
inspect subgoal_gap or inv_from_lemma for invariant shape
downgrade this route and choose another current-view navigation item

moves.title: Proof-route context
moves.guidance: Probabilistic VC plan: query-counter loss accounting via have-chain, rewrite Pr/mu rules, real-order smt
moves.applicability: Use this as route-selection context; it is not a tactic to run as-is.

checks: Pr resources are context: use them when they match the visible endpoint and simplify the goal.
Direct byequiv is a valid route when the goal is naturally a program equivalence.

budget_ledger.read_as: A probability budget ledger: factor slots are the resources in the product bound, and event obligations are the proof events that should spend those resources before local sampling cleanup.

likely_proof_family: split the event/budget before descending into procedure proof

useful_fact_handles.why: The group order denominator appears in the probability budget.
The query-bound numerator appears in the probability budget.
Product-bound side conditions often need non-negativity of factors.
Ratio factors in the bound usually need numerator/denominator non-negativity.
Powered ratio factors often need exponent non-negativity closure.
Strict positivity is useful when rewriting ratio-over-itself side conditions.
Strictly positive denominator/numerator facts support positive ratio factors.
Powered positive ratio factors stay positive.
B/B residuals often close by rewriting `divrr` after proving B is nonzero.
Membership-event probability bounds often use list-size or union-bound style lemmas when available.

side_condition_recipe.name: B_over_B residual
side_condition_recipe.read_as: When a residual becomes a budget divided by the same budget, treat it as B/B: establish B <> 0 or B > 0, then rewrite with `divrr`.

facts_and_diagnostics.fact_note: Only current-frontier facts are shown here; the full proof context artifact keeps the compiler/internal details.

probability_budget.read_as: Advisory middle-end context for route selection; this is not a proof script or tactic order.

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

ask_manager_for.use_when: Goal is a Pr inequality; EC native search can retrieve probability upper-bound lemmas while staying at the Pr layer.
ask_manager_for.returns: manager-provided read-only context for this proof route

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need parsed goal shape, resolved names, or KB pattern hints.
Current Pr equality may need a manager-verified bridge route (game-hop or scheme/endpoint normalization) before direct byequiv/proc lowering.
ask_manager_for.returns: daemon-verified, committable Pr bridge routes with manager-owned intent payloads

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need pRHL/procedure-equivalence bridge lemma names or context after checking pr_bridge_routes.
ask_manager_for.returns: context-only equiv bridge lemma candidates (names to look up/apply); not a verified Pr route

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need local lemma hints before choosing a Pr-level proof route.
ask_manager_for.returns: nearby lemma hints and proof-route context

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need exact byphoare/phoare-loop tactic syntax before probing the probability route.
